source: Remediation sequence C1-C6 (fresh-eyes audit 2026-06-06); filed for later manual implementation. See docs/PROJECT_MAP.md and the approved plan.
title: Extract a StorageBackend seam (transport vs tier logic) (C1/C6)
what_failed: Tier logic interleaves with raw transport (sqlite3.connect, self.supabase.table) inside the 2,265-line memory god-object, so storage and tier bugs are entangled and hard to test in isolation.
where_failed: tools/tools_memory.py - sqlite3 + supabase calls scattered through tier methods.
migration_plan: 1. Define a StorageBackend with insert/select/update per store (SQLite l3_cache, Supabase tables).
2. The read/write cores (T-163/164) call the backend instead of touching sqlite3/supabase directly.
3. Inject a fake backend in tests to exercise tier logic with no DB at all.
4. verify.py green; memory layer now unit-testable without a real store.
implementation_notes: Smart and simple: a thin transport seam, not an ORM. This IS the C6 memory-god-object split - do it here, once.
analysis: Separating transport from tier logic makes the memory layer testable in isolation and shrinks the god-object as a side effect.
